2605.07432.title: Generating training datasets for legal chatbots in Korean
2605.07432.paper_summary: Chatbots are robots that can communicate with humans using text or voice signals. Legal chatbots improve access to justice, since legal representation and legal advice by lawyers come with a high cost that excludes disadvantaged and vulnerable people. However, capturing the diversity of actual user input in datasets for deep-learning dialog systems (chatbots) is a technical challenge. Diversity requires large volumes of data, which must also be labelled in order to classify the user's intent, while the cost of labelling datasets increases with volume. Instead of labelling large volumes of authentic data from users, our approach consists in jointly generating large volumes of utterances and high-quality labels. The generator of labelled datasets is based on language resources that take the form of local grammar graphs (LGG), which capture and generalize the vocabulary and local syntax observed by linguists in text. The LGGs associate labels to the utterances according to a domain-specific classification system. We tested this approach by implementing LIGA, a legal chatbot in Korean. The chatbot answers users' conversational queries on legal situations by providing information on similar legal cases, made publicly available by the Korean government. We generated labelled utterances from the LGGs with the aid of the open-source Unitex platform. This process produced 700 million utterances. We trained a DIET classifier on a dataset made of these utterances, and the trained model reached 91% f1-score performance. We implemented a chatbot called LIGA, which uses the results of the model to select a link to a web page that documents similar legal cases.
2605.07432.paper_summary_zh: 聊天機器人是可以使用文本或語音信號與人類溝通的機器人。法律聊天機器人改善了對正義的獲取，因為律師的法律代表和法律建議成本高昂，使弱勢和脆弱群體無法獲得服務。然而，在深度學習對話系統（聊天機器人）的數據集中捕捉實際用戶輸入的多樣性是一項技術挑戰。多樣性需要大量數據，而這些數據必須標記以便分類用戶的意圖，而標記數據集的成本隨著數量的增加而上升。我們的方法不是標記大量來自用戶的真實數據，而是共同生成大量的話語和高質量的標籤。標記數據集的生成器基於語言資源，這些資源以本地語法圖（LGG）的形式存在，捕捉並概括語言學家在文本中觀察到的詞彙和本地語法。LGG根據特定領域的分類系統將標籤與話語關聯。我們通過實施LIGA，一個韓語的法律聊天機器人，測試了這種方法。該聊天機器人通過提供有關相似法律案件的信息來回答用戶對法律情況的對話查詢，這些信息由韓國政府公開提供。我們在開源的Unitex平台的幫助下，從LGG生成了標記的話語。這個過程產生了7億個話語。我們在由這些話語組成的數據集上訓練了一個DIET分類器，訓練後的模型達到了91%的f1-score表現。我們實施了一個名為LIGA的聊天機器人，該聊天機器人利用模型的結果選擇一個鏈接，指向記錄相似法律案件的網頁。
2605.07432.author: Changhoe Hwang et.al.
2605.07432.authors: Changhoe Hwang, Jee-Sun Nam, Eric Laporte
